description: An evaluation of multi-stage legally-grounded recursive reasoning. Solvers must parse semi-structured, legally-phrased bilateral trade treaties, trace MFN tariff updates and category hierarchies across recursive propagation rounds, and calculate final stable tariffs.
capability_claim: Tests advanced LLMs' ability to accurately interpret complex, legally-phrased conditional rules and follow recursive, stateful updates to a stable mathematical fixed point or stalemate lock, a task requiring high-fidelity reading comprehension and precise sequential logic.

closest_benchmarks.name: GPQA
closest_benchmarks.difference: While GPQA evaluates factual domain-expert graduate science, MFN-Cascade evaluates procedural reasoning under dense, legally-phrased logical dependencies and recursive loops.

closest_benchmarks.name: LiveCodeBench / HumanEval
closest_benchmarks.difference: Instead of evaluating code generation, MFN-Cascade evaluates code-execution-like procedural trace reasoning directly on dense textual specifications without allowing models to simply copy-paste a pre-written interpreter.

closest_benchmarks.name: OSWorld / WebArena
closest_benchmarks.difference: MFN-Cascade is self-contained and does not require complex UI or browser interaction, focusing purely on deep logical inference and multi-step propagation.
